text: Program evaluation involves the systematic collection of data about the implementation and operation of policies and other programmes and is fundamental to all collective activity. The present study employed literature review from educational e-books and journals to develop a framework of educational evaluation that can be used by teachers to evaluate the programmes in schools. The main objective of the study was to empower teachers with the necessary skills and knowledge required to carry out programme assessment in schools efficiently. It established that the evaluation of previous school programmes is crucial in assessing school performance and addressing areas requiring improvement. Moreover, the integration of current educational policies as standards of reference provided teachers and inspectors with an effective tool for evaluating past teaching methods and thereby planning for future improvements in instructional programmes.
